rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are visible in the current scope or properly declared as helpers.
Use only valid AADL feature names and declared auxiliary variables in implementation-level AGREE assignments and guarantees.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only visible identifiers or previously declared helpers within the same annex scope.
Assignments in implementation scopes should target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Type mismatches in AGREE expressions must be resolved by aligning helper declarations with their usage types.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes are for bindings and assignments.
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use only assignments for implementation-level bindings.
Do not reference identifiers or types that are not declared or visible in the current AGREE annex.
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE validator diagnostics and repair strategies
Component type vs implementation scope boundaries in AADL
